preamble: The shift from a lateral affricate [tɬ] to an alveolar fricative [s] is a process of assibilation and delateralization. This simplification of complex lateral sounds into common sibilants is a common path in lateral weakening systems.

examples.note: Characteristic of Mexican Spanish influences on local dialects where the distinct lateral affricate is replaced by a simple sibilant.
Historical developments in some branches of the family show the reduction of lateral affricates into simple sibilants.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
